claude.message: fix: Capture Claude: lines in User/Action response cards

added: if (boldOnly && (inExCard || inUaCard || (i + 3 < lines.length && lines.slice(i+1, i+4).some(function(l) { return l.trim().match(/^`<(example_user_memories|user)|^(User|Action|Claude):/); })))) {
removed: if (boldOnly && (inExCard || inUaCard || (i + 3 < lines.length && lines.slice(i+1, i+4).some(function(l) { return l.trim().match(/^`<(example_user_memories|user)|^(User|Action):/); })))) {

claude.summary: The change updates the system to recognize lines starting with "Claude:" in addition to "User:" and "Action:". This allows the system to correctly identify and format Claude's lines in the response cards.

claude.message: feat: Redesign claude.html with Anthropic docs aesthetic
claude.summary: This change updates the design of CLAUDE's system prompt to match Anthropic's style, making it look more modern and sleek. The update includes new formatting for tables of contents, preference cards, and other visual elements to improve the overall user experience.

claude.message: feat: Add favicon to Claude System Prompt Explorer
claude.summary: This change adds a small icon, called a favicon, to the browser tab for CLAUDE's System Prompt Explorer. It's a tiny image that shows up next to the page title, and it's been added using a line of code that links to an "favicon.ico" file. This gives the page a bit more visual personality and makes it easier to spot in your browser tabs.

added: The user's name is GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_NAME , and their email address is HANDLE@gmail.com.
removed: The user's name is Ásgeir Thor Johnson, and their email address is asgeirtj@gmail.com.

gemini.summary: Someone updated the system prompt to change the user's name and email address. The old name was Ásgeir Thor Johnson, and the new name is now just a placeholder called GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_NAME. The email address was also changed to a generic HANDLE@gmail.com.

grok.summary: GROK updated its system to change how it searches the web and looks for specific keywords. The update didn't really add or remove any major features, but it did tweak how these search tools are defined and work behind the scenes. Overall, it's a pretty minor change that shouldn't affect how you use GROK's search tools.
